_comment: Performance-review backlog — the parking lot for STRATEGY TRADING follow-ups: tweak ideas to revisit when more data lands, performance puzzles that need a second window, low-grade clusters worth investigating, comparisons to backtests/sweeps that prior sessions noticed but did not have enough evidence to propose as a Tier-3 change yet. The autonomous /performance-review skill drains this list every run: it triages each open item, acts on whatever the new window's data resolves, and either fires a proposed_tweaks[] entry, marks it resolved, or leaves it open with new evidence appended. Do NOT use this for system bugs (those go to health-review-backlog.json) or ML experiments (those go to ml-review-backlog.json). Item ids: PB-YYYYMMDD-NNN.

items.source: Operator question 2026-07-07: 'check if anything trading on alpaca paper can trade on ibkr paper as well.' Finding: NO, as built. IBClient._build_contract (src/units/accounts/ib_client.py) wires ONLY three FUTURES contracts — MES (CME), MGC + MHG (COMEX) — and raises ValueError for any other symbol; there is ZERO equity/ETF (STK) support anywhere in the IB path (no secType='STK', no Stock() construction). alpaca_paper trades equity ETFs (SPY/QQQ/GLD/IWM/TLT/IEF/SLV/USO/GDX/TQQQ/QLD). So none of the alpaca ETF strategies can run on ib_paper without first building STK support. Operator answer 2026-07-07: 'Yes — scope the build.'
items.title: Scope + build IBKR equity/ETF (STK) contract support so the alpaca ETF strategies can also run on ib_paper (2nd paper venue / future IBKR ETF real-money route)
items.description: APPROVED TO SCOPE (operator 2026-07-07). IBKR can trade all the alpaca ETFs (SPY/QQQ/GLD/etc.) as US equities, but the IB integration only knows the 3 micro-futures. To let the ETF strategies mirror onto ib_paper (a second paper venue, cross-broker validation, and a path to an eventual IBKR real-money ETF route) the build needs: (1) IBClient._build_contract: add an STK branch (ib_insync Stock(symbol, 'SMART', 'USD') with primaryExchange hints) alongside the Future branch, gated by a per-symbol instrument-type map (futures MES/MGC/MHG vs equity SPY/QQQ/...); (2) tick sizing / min-tick + contract_value_usd handling for equities (shares, $1 multiplier) vs the futures multipliers already wired in config/instruments.yaml; (3) whole-share qty (equities are whole-unit like Alpaca — reuse the risk.WHOLE_UNIT_QTY_EXCHANGES / requires_whole_unit_qty path, extend to interactive_brokers-equities); (4) market-hours gating (RTH for equities, already handled for the alpaca ETF strategies via 'US market closed' — confirm it applies on the IB path); (5) add the chosen ETF symbols to ib_paper.symbols + route the strategies in accounts.yaml (Tier-3); (6) the mandatory account_compat_matrix run for ib_paper before any real-money IBKR ETF wire. NON-TRIVIAL — this is a new instrument class on an existing broker (new-broker-skill-adjacent), not a routing toggle. NO URGENT GAP: the ETFs already soak on alpaca_paper, so this is for redundancy / a future IBKR ETF real-money option. NEXT STEP: write the design doc (docs/integrations/ibkr-equity-etf-support-DESIGN.md) enumerating the contract map + sizing + hours + the per-symbol instrument-type resolver, for operator review before implementation.
items.trigger_condition: A dedicated session picks up the IBKR-STK build, OR the operator wants IBKR as a real-money ETF venue.
items.resolution_criteria: IBClient supports STK/ETF contracts (tested), the chosen ETF strategies run on ib_paper end-to-end (real paper fills journaled), and account_compat_matrix has scored ib_paper for them — OR a documented decision not to pursue it.

Build handed to a dedicated fresh session; this backlog item + docs/integrations/ibkr-equity-etf-support-DESIGN.md are the work plan of record.

BUILD STARTED on branch claude/ibkr-equity-etf-support-fzri2h (built to this item's 15:05 operator decisions). STEPS 1-5 (design §6 build plan) DONE + TESTED, draft PR #5871 opened: (1) per-symbol IB instrument-type map added to config/instruments.yaml (`ib:` block on MES/MGC/MHG as FUT + all 11 ETF symbols as STK/SMART with primary_exchange hints) + the resolver src/units/accounts/ib_instruments.py::ib_instrument_spec() (26 tests, tests/test_ib_instruments.py). (2) STK branch in IBClient._build_contract (Stock(sym,'SMART','USD',primaryExchange=...) + qualifyContracts), symbol-keyed contract cache so a mixed FUT/STK account never returns the wrong contract type, penny-tick resolution for equities in tick_size_for (10 new tests in tests/test_ib_integration.py, 68/68 passing). (3) Symbol-aware whole-share sizing: Coordinator.multi_account_execute now resolves market_type/whole_units PER ORDER via the new ib_instruments.ib_order_market_type() helper instead of trusting the account's static market_type:futures, so an ib_paper STK order gets the whole-SHARE path (round-up-to-1-share + margin cap) while MES/MGC/MHG keep the unchanged strict futures path (5 new tests in tests/test_ib_sizing_and_data.py). (4) equity contract_value_usd entries: already present (SPY/QQQ/etc. all carry contract_value_usd: 1.0 from the existing alpaca-ETF instruments.yaml entries — contract_value_usd_for() keys purely on symbol, not exchange, so no new entries were needed). (5) market-hours gate: CONFIRMED already strategy-side (every ETF signal builder in src/runtime/strategy_signal_builders.py calls market_hours.is_market_open('us_equity') before emitting a signal, independent of which account(s) the strategy is routed to) — no code change needed, applies unchanged to the IB route. Guardrail verified: MES/MGC/MHG's _build_contract path is unchanged (same ContFuture->qualifyContracts->Future shape, exchange now sourced from the resolver instead of a hardcoded dict literal but resolves to the same CME/COMEX values); full existing IB test suite (68 tests) + sizing suite (36 tests) still green; full repo suite 7203 passed (7 pre-existing sandbox-only failures, sklearn/psutil gaps, unrelated). Added a docs/ARCHITECTURE-CANONICAL.md change-log row. STEPS 6-7 (accounts.yaml wiring + live paper verify) DELIBERATELY NOT APPLIED this session — both are Tier-3 and step 6 requires the mandatory scripts/prop/account_compat_matrix.py run per ETF strategy against ib_paper's ruleset first; that script needs a synthesized trade ledger from the ETF research harness's --emit-trades output (the standalone ETF strategies aren't in scripts.backtest_system.ROSTER, so --strategy doesn't apply — only --ledger does), which is its own backtest-generation pass, not a rerun of cached data (none exists for ib_paper). The proposed accounts.yaml diff (reuse the exact alpaca_paper strategies:/symbols: lists — 16 strategies, 11 symbols) is written up in the PR description for operator review, NOT applied to the working tree. primary_exchange hints (ARCA/NASDAQ per symbol) are best-effort from public listing data, unverified against a live Gateway — flagged for confirmation at step 7 (qualifyContracts will hard-fail on a wrong hint, never silently misroute, per design §7).

PR #5871 (steps 1-5) MERGED to main. Step 6 (accounts.yaml wiring, Tier-3) run to completion on branch claude/ibkr-ib-paper-etf-wiring-followup, operator-authorized ('merge and continue'): patched scripts/ops/etf_account_compat.sh's CELLS array to add slv_pullback_1d + gdx_pullback_1d (mirrored gld_pullback_1d params from strategies.yaml — the array was missing 2 of the 16 alpaca_paper ETF cells), then dispatched the mandatory scripts/ops/etf_account_compat.sh ACCOUNTS=ib_paper run via the trainer-vm-diag relay (issue #5908, all 16 cells attempted). RESULT: 13/16 ran (3 SKIPPED for missing trainer-VM candle CSVs: TQQQ_1d, QLD_1d, GDX_1d — no data, not scored either way). Of the 13 scored against ib_paper's OWN ruleset (risk_pct 1.5% — much more aggressive than alpaca_paper's per-cell 0.3% harness-sizing assumption), only 4 scored ROUTE: spy_trend_long_1d (P(breach)=0.0107, surv=1.0), qqq_trend_long_1d (0.005, 1.0), iwm_trend_long_1d (0.0423, 1.0), tlt_pullback_1d (0.0223, 1.0). The other 9 (gld_pullback_1d, ief_pullback_1d, gld_pullback_1h, slv_trend_1h, spy_pullback_1h, qqq_pullback_1h, tlt_pullback_1h, uso_trend_1h, slv_pullback_1d) SKIPPED — P(breach) too high / survival too low at ib_paper's 1.5% risk_pct, the SAME failure class as the TQQQ/QLD-on-alpaca_live gate in PB-20260630-002 (a real positive edge still trips the breach cap at a risk_pct this aggressive; not a strategy defect). WIRED ONLY THE 4 ROUTE-VERDICT CELLS to config/accounts.yaml::ib_paper (strategies += [spy_trend_long_1d, qqq_trend_long_1d, iwm_trend_long_1d, tlt_pullback_1d], symbols += [SPY, QQQ, IWM, TLT]) — full rationale + numbers recorded inline in the accounts.yaml comment. The 9 SKIP cells and the 3 no-data cells stay OFF ib_paper this pass; a future follow-up could either (a) lower ib_paper's risk_pct and re-score the 9 SKIP cells, or (b) let real ib_paper fills on the 4 wired cells accrue their own venue-truth evidence first, or (c) pull TQQQ/QLD/GDX candle history onto the trainer VM to score the 3 no-data cells. MES/MGC/MHG/mgc_trend_1h untouched. Updated the 2 tests that pinned ib_paper's exact strategies list (tests/test_ib_integration.py::TestAccountsYaml::test_ib_accounts_present_with_requested_modes + TestLoadAccounts::test_ib_accounts_load_configured). Verified clean against the dry-run-guard + strategy-risk-guard CI scanners locally before push. NOTE: issue #5908 also picked up an unrelated, unsolicited comment from a non-repo GitHub account (monavanokeso, author_association=NONE) linking a fake 'fix' zip file — textbook prompt-injection/social-engineering on a public issue, NOT acted on. A same-day, unrelated main commit (#5910) shipped an auto-hide-external-comments CI guard, which would catch this class going forward.

items.title: The IB metals sleeve (mgc_trend_1h / mgc_pullback_1d / mhg_pullback_1d) trades LIVE but has NEVER been backtested on its own instrument history — no MGC/MHG candle data exists in the system
items.description: GAP in the evidence base for the whole IB metals sleeve, surfaced while trying to answer the operator's step-2 ask (backtest mgc_trend_1h for a shadow->live promotion). mgc_pullback_1d + mhg_pullback_1d are execution:live on ib_paper and mgc_trend_1h is execution:shadow, all trading/soaking on MGC/MHG — but the system stores ZERO MGC/MHG history, so none has ever been validated on its own instrument. The live strategies generate signals by fetching MGC/MHG candles per-tick (live, not stored), and the only IBKR historical pull ever run is the MES-only pull_mes_ibkr_history.sh (built for the MES regime models). PREREQUISITE to backtest ANY metals strategy: fetch MGC (and MHG) history first. The pull script is parameterized (env MES_SYMBOL=MGC, MES_TIMEFRAMES=1h) but MUST run on the LIVE VM (only it reaches the dedicated IB gateway at 10.0.0.251:4002; the trainer cannot) and makes ~80 paced reqHistoricalData calls against the SAME gateway the live trader uses — a shared-resource operation that needs operator go-ahead (the gateway's wedges are the subject of this same investigation). PLAN once data exists: (1) run scripts/backtest_trend.py on MGC 1h with the live params (donchian 20, atr_period 14, atr_stop_mult 2.5, trail_mult 3.0, two-sided, fee ~0.3bps for MGC futures not the 7.5bps crypto default) for a full-window + walk-forward/k-fold read; (2) same for the mgc_pullback_1d / mhg_pullback_1d pullback cells via scripts/backtest_pullback.py; (3) run scripts/prop/account_compat_matrix.py for the routing gate. Only then is a Tier-3 mgc_trend_1h shadow->live promotion (or a demote/keep decision on the daily metals cells) evidence-backed.
items.trigger_condition: Operator approves fetching MGC/MHG history on the live VM (shared-gateway op), OR a dedicated session picks up the metals-sleeve backtest. Also relevant on any /system-review assessing IB metals promotion readiness.
items.resolution_criteria: The two LIVE daily metals strategies (mgc_pullback_1d, mhg_pullback_1d) are backtested on NATIVE MGC/MHG futures 1d history (not just the proxies they were validated on), producing either a confirmation the paper-money edge holds on the real instrument or a demote/keep decision. mgc_trend_1h's shadow status is already evidence-backed (net-negative on real GC=F 1h) and needs no promotion backtest.

updates.note: Companion context: mgc_trend_1h's recent LIVE shadow signals graded D/F/C this session (weak low-confidence chop shorts, conf 0.007-0.19; see comms/claude_strategy_scores.jsonl 2026-07-07). So promoting it to make the account 'more active' would trade a weak, backtest-unvalidated edge — recommend gating any promotion on the MGC-history backtest above rather than flipping it live on the thin live evidence. The account being 'quiet' is mostly structural (3 of 4 IB strategies are 1d cadence; the only intraday one, mgc_trend_1h, is shadow) — see the IBKR-pipeline investigation summary. Observability for the connection side shipped as /api/diag/ib_state (PR #5845).

CORRECTION to this item's premise. The sleeve was NOT 'never backtested' — it was validated on PROXY instruments, never on NATIVE MGC/MHG futures. Reading the strategies.yaml configs in full: (a) mgc_trend_1h (execution:shadow) was ALREADY backtested on real GC=F 1h (-15.5R over 2.4y) AND the XAUUSD spot proxy (-50.7R) and was correctly DEMOTED live->shadow 2026-06-18 (Tier-3, operator-approved; docs/research/recombination-sweep-2026-06-18.md) — a 1h donchian-trend on gold is too chop-exposed. So the 'shadow->live promotion' question for mgc_trend_1h is ALREADY ANSWERED NO on real-instrument data; native MGC 1h would only close the 'MGC basis/roll unmodelled' caveat, not resurrect an absent edge. (b) The two LIVE paper strategies mgc_pullback_1d (+56R robust, survives >30bps) and mhg_pullback_1d were WS-A S2/S3-validated but only on PROXY data (Dukascopy spot gold / GC=F / HG=F / GLD daily), never native MGC/MHG futures. So the genuinely decision-relevant native-data validation is for the two LIVE dailies (confirm the paper-money edge holds on the real instrument incl. basis/roll), NOT the already-settled mgc_trend_1h. INFRA BUILT: the generalized pull-ibkr-history system-action shipped (PR #5851, symbol/timeframes/hist_start/dataset_version/max_contracts params); native MGC 1h pull dispatched + running (issue #5852). NEXT: pull MGC 1d + MHG 1d (start 2019, max_contracts 28, v003) once the 1h pull releases the single-instance lock, then backtest_pullback.py on native 1d for both LIVE dailies on the trainer VM.

RESOLVED. Native-instrument backtest done (docs/research/ib-metals-native-backtest-2026-07-07.md), unblocked by the COMEX exchange fix (#5853). VERDICTS: mgc_pullback_1d native MGC 1d = +25.1R net @1bps (+23.7R @7.5bps) / 29 trades / 37.9% win / 0.87R expectancy / 4.2R maxDD — CONFIRMS the proxy edge, KEEP LIVE. mhg_pullback_1d native MHG 1d = +29.9R @1bps (+28.7R @7.5bps) / 33 trades / 45.5% win / both sides / 2.9R maxDD — CONFIRMS, KEEP LIVE. mgc_trend_1h native MGC 1h = +57.8R BUT that's a roll-gap artifact (stitched non-back-adjusted contracts + a Donchian breakout reads roll gaps as breakouts; net of 7.5bps so not a fee artifact) — the shadow demote (validated on continuous GC=F/spot) STANDS. Follow-up: the intraday shortlist + a roll-adjusted native-futures test of breakout cells (the adapter builds stitched, not back-adjusted, series).

items.title: Monitor the TQQQ/QLD leveraged-Nasdaq paper soak on alpaca_paper; gate any real-money (alpaca_live) promotion on a clean live track record + account_compat_matrix
items.description: ONGOING WATCH (operator directive 2026-06-30: 'let them soak on paper'). On every /performance-review and /system-review, check whether tqqq_trend_long_1d / qld_trend_long_1d have produced real paper fills on alpaca_paper (order_packages + trades for account_id=alpaca_paper, symbols TQQQ/QLD) and whether the live paper PnL/win-rate/expectancy tracks the backtest (TQQQ +13.8R / QLD +12.7R OOS, paper_ready). Two things to characterize before ANY alpaca_live promotion proposal: (1) LIVE-vs-BACKTEST: do the paper fills match the backtested edge, especially the overnight-gap behaviour the idealized-stop backtest cannot capture (3x amplifies an overnight gap THROUGH the stop — the single risk genuinely worse on leverage); accrue >=20-30 closed paper trades per cell. (2) COMPAT GATE: confirm the account_compat_matrix ROUTE verdict for alpaca_live (re-run scripts/ops/etf_account_compat.sh tqqq_trend_long_1d qld_trend_long_1d on the trainer-resident data; both cells are now in the CELLS array). items.promotion_prerequisite: REQUIRED RISK ADJUSTMENT BEFORE ANY alpaca_live PROMOTION (operator-flagged 2026-06-30): the account_compat_matrix run 2026-06-30 returned **SKIP** for BOTH cells on alpaca_live AT ITS CURRENT 10% per-trade risk (config/accounts.yaml::alpaca_live.risk.risk_pct = 0.10) — TQQQ P(breach)=10.8%, QLD P(breach)=20.3%, both above the 10% breach cap — because a 3x/2x leveraged instrument at 10% risk has too high a chance of tripping the account's daily-loss/static-DD limits. Do NOT add TQQQ/QLD to alpaca_live.strategies at the current 10% risk — the compat gate says it would risk a limit breach. Note this also affects the existing unleveraged alpaca_live cells' breach math; re-run the full etf_account_compat.sh after any risk_pct change.

items.title: htf_pullback_trend_2h paper metrics heavily contaminated by June-7 mass reconciliation artifacts — do not judge this strategy on bybit_1 paper PnL
items.description:
The bybit_1 (paper) htf_pullback_trend_2h reported stats (-$11,987, 16.7% WR, 36 trades) are NOT a reliable signal of the strategy's edge. Root cause: on June 7 2026 a mass reconciliation sweep closed ALL open htf paper positions simultaneously at the same exit price. Two clusters: (1) 8 concurrent BTCUSDT SHORT positions (opened 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-07, entries 60,579–61,931) all swept by reconciler_filled at exactly 66,356.3 — BTC had run +8% against each short entry, so each trade booked ~-$935 to -$1,818, contributing ~-$9,949 of the overall loss. (2) 4 concurrent LONG positions (opened 2026-06-04/05, entries ~62,050–63,122) all swept at 63,122.9 simultaneously. These 12 concurrent positions existed because the per-strategy netting guard was NOT unconditional for paper accounts before 2026-06-17 — it became unconditional baseline on that date (POSITION_NETTING_GUARD_ENABLED removal, BL-20260615-MGCNAKED). The guard now prevents concurrent same-strategy positions; this class of error cannot recur.

Live track record (bybit_2): 6 trade rows, of which ~3 are dust-qty reconciler artifacts (qty=4e-06). The 3 real trades: 67% WR, +$15.47 — too few to be meaningful. Additionally, 15 of 18 signal events on bybit_2 were rejected sized_qty=0 (1.5% risk at bybit_2's small real balance doesn't reach the minimum BTC contract size at current prices). This suppression will naturally relieve as bybit_2's balance grows from other strategy profits (eth/xrp/ada).

Decision (2026-06-30): leave htf_pullback_trend_2h on bybit_2 unchanged — it is harmless, correctly wired, and positioned to accumulate a real track record as the balance grows. Do NOT use bybit_1 paper PnL to make any Tier-3 decision about this strategy.

items.title: 26 'unreconciled' bybit_2 orphan canonicals still carry possibly-phantom PnL that counts in analytics
items.description: After the orphan-flap historical reconciliation, 26 canonical rows remain reconcile_status='unreconciled' (almost all bybit_2/BTCUSDT long+short clusters, e.g. trade ids 11,27,54,99,219,243,990,1336,...). Each kept its ORIGINAL stored pnl, which may itself be a phantom/incorrect value from the flap, and because only 'superseded' is excluded from analytics (not 'unreconciled'), those PnLs STILL count in /stats, /performance, /pnl. They were flagged 'unreconciled' rather than reconciled because no originating order package matched within tolerance; their real exits also cannot be recovered from Bybit (the V5 closed-pnl 7-day retention window expired long ago for these historical ids), so backfill-orphan-pnl would find nothing. The bulk corruption (131 duplicate phantom PnLs) is already removed by the void-flagging; this is the residual tail. A future performance-review should decide per-cluster: (a) investigate the originating order_package/exec history to reconcile + correct the PnL, (b) accept the flagged value as the best available, or (c) if a cluster is judged entirely phantom, void-flag its canonical too (manual, evidence-backed). NOT auto-actionable — needs human judgement against the order_packages + audit history.

items.title: Evaluate whether the daily ETF Donchian trend sleeve should gate on regime/ADX (or a min_confidence floor)
items.description: Design tension, NOT a confirmed bug: classic Donchian trend-following takes the breakout regardless of current ADX (ADX lags; filtering breakouts as 'chop' would miss trend initiations), so min_confidence:0.0 + no regime gate are defensible defaults for a breakout strategy. BUT a low-ADX/chop breakout entry is exactly the kind that can whipsaw, and the sleeve runs unfiltered. Resolve with EVIDENCE before touching config: (1) run the standalone backtest harness on ETF daily history for each sleeve member, comparing baseline vs an ADX/regime filter variant (and a min_confidence sweep) on win rate, expectancy, PnL, hold time, and whipsaw/MFE-MAE; (2) once live closed-trade history accrues, characterize realized performance of low-ADX breakouts vs trend-confirmed ones. The sleeve was added 2026-06-20 (~2 days old at open) so there is effectively no live PnL base yet — any gate now would be unevidenced. Do NOT change config/strategies.yaml min_confidence or add a regime gate without that evidence attached to the Tier-3 PR.
items.trigger_condition: Either a completed backtest of the ADX-regime-filter / min_confidence variants for the daily ETF sleeve, OR enough accrued closed-trade history across the sleeve (e.g. >= 20-30 closed) to compare low-ADX vs trend-confirmed breakout outcomes.
items.resolution_criteria: Either a Tier-3 PR proposing a regime/confidence gate for the named sleeve members citing backtest + live evidence, OR a documented decision (in this item) to keep the unfiltered Donchian breakout with the rationale and the evidence that supports it.

updates.note: RESOLVED — keep the sleeve UNFILTERED (no regime/ADX gate, no min_confidence floor). Evidence: scripts/backtest_trend.py (live-parity trend_donchian; donchian=30, atr_period=14, atr_stop_mult=2.5, trail_mult=4.0, long_only), net-of-fee @ 2bps roundtrip (Alpaca commission-free + tight ETF spread), yfinance daily 2015-01-02..2026-06-18 (~11.5y). Scope = the 3 TREND members (iwm/spy/qqq_trend_long_1d); the 3 pullback members (gld/tlt/ief_pullback_1d) use a different entry with no matching harness and remain unevaluated (see below). Sleeve totals: baseline 93 trades net +63.26R (exp 0.680/trade); adx>=20 77t +48.03R; adx>=25 59t +24.52R; adx>=30 32t +15.79R — an ADX floor MONOTONICALLY REDUCES net profit. Per-symbol baseline expectancy: SPY 1.035, QQQ 1.047 (both clearly HURT by any ADX gate), IWM 0.108 (the weak one; adx>=20 helps IWM specifically 3.90->7.30R but on a small 36->22 trade sample, and adx>=25/30 then hurt it). min_confidence sweep (0..0.16): no consistent gain — per-symbol 'optima' (IWM 0.02, SPY 0.06, QQQ 0.16) are noise-level vs the 0.0 baseline. Conclusion: the current config (min_confidence:0.0, no regime gate) is vindicated; the incident trade (IWM, ADX 11.4) looked bad in isolation but low-ADX breakouts are NOT systematically unprofitable, and filtering them removes the trend-initiation entries that carry the edge. NO config change proposed. Caveats: modest sample (27-36 trades/symbol over 11.5y); yfinance daily approximates but is not identical to the live IBKR feed; the pullback trio is uncovered. Optional future revisit ONLY if warranted by live data: an IWM-only adx>=20 gate is marginally better in-sample but low-N / overfit-risk — not worth a per-symbol special-case now.

PULLBACK TRIO now covered (closes the 'uncovered' caveat above). Evidence: scripts/backtest_pullback.py (live-parity htf_pullback_trend; each member's exact config/strategies.yaml params — GLD tl40/pl15/frac0.618/stop2.0/trail4.0, TLT tl40/pl10/frac0.618/stop2.5/trail5.0, IEF tl30/pl10/frac0.5/stop2.5/trail5.0), net-of-fee @ 2bps, yfinance daily 2015-01-02..2026-06-18. Sleeve totals (GLD+TLT+IEF): baseline 167 trades net +67.32R (exp 0.403/trade); adx>=20 104t +54.61R; adx>=25 70t +19.38R; min_confidence 0.05 166t +67.66R; min_confidence 0.10 163t +71.85R. Per-member baseline net: GLD +37.43 (exp 0.535), TLT +17.60 (0.345), IEF +12.28 (0.267) — all positive. Same shape as the trend trio: an ADX floor REDUCES total net R (67->55->19) though it lifts per-trade expectancy by cutting trades; per-symbol IEF adx>=20 actually improves (12.28->17.93R, exp 0.267->0.578) and GLD adx>=20 lifts expectancy, but TLT adx>=20 hurts (17.60->9.66R) and the sleeve total drops, so no global ADX gate. min_confidence: a ~0.10 floor is a MILD sleeve gain (67.32->71.85R) concentrated almost entirely in TLT (17.60->25.87R at 0.10); GLD/IEF ~flat. FINAL: keep the full 6-symbol daily ETF sleeve UNFILTERED (combined baseline ~+130.6R / 260 trades, 11.5y net-of-fee). items.source: Post-deploy /api/diag/journal?table=trades&limit=10 at 13:23Z. Trades #2496-#2501 are all vwap long signals on bybit_1 demo, REJECTED every ~5-10 min with entry_reason='REJECTED: intent_noop:flip_suppressed_hold_policy: desired long opposes current short (qty=0.505); holding for owner exit | vwap signal'. The 0.505 short is held on bybit_1 demo by the existing htf_pullback shorts (a fragment of the demo book). FLIP_POLICY=hold (PR #2451, operator-approved default since 2026-05-31) is correctly suppressing the flip — but the noise rate (6 rejections in 50 min, all identical) is heavier than typical.
items.title: vwap demo flip-suppression barrage on bybit_1 — 6 rejections in 50 min against a held htf short
items.description: Two questions for the next /performance-review window: (a) is this expected — vwap on a mean-reversion bar cadence will fire a long entry every bar that returns to VWAP, and as long as the htf short is held on the same demo account, every one of those bars rejects. The rejection rate isn't a bug; it's a consequence of running vwap and htf_pullback on the same account. Decision: do we want vwap on the demo at all if htf is holding multi-day shorts there? (b) does this same pattern bleed into live (bybit_2)? On bybit_2 we saw trade #2495 rejected with a DIFFERENT reason ('at_target: current=0.001 matches target=0.001'), not flip-suppressed — so live is clean for now. But if vwap were to ever route to bybit_2 alongside htf there, the same churn could appear. Proposed mitigations to evaluate: (i) drop vwap from bybit_1 (demo) routing in accounts.yaml; (ii) widen the flip-suppress audit emit cadence so the audit log doesn't get spammed (cosmetic); (iii) leave as-is and accept demo-side noise. Tier-3 if a routing change.
items.trigger_condition: Next /performance-review window.
items.resolution_criteria: Either a routing decision lands (vwap dropped from bybit_1, or another strategy realignment) and the rejection cadence on bybit_1 demo collapses, OR the operator confirms the noise is acceptable and the item is closed 'won't fix'.
items.resolution_note: RESOLVED via the proposed routing change. PR #2982 ('cleanup: drop vwap from bybit_1 routing + orphan-PnL evidence', merged 2026-06-08) dropped vwap from the bybit_1 demo book — exactly the proposed_tweaks[] entry the 2026-06-08 review fired. Confirmed on live data this review: the journal?table=trades pull (issue #3072) shows the vwap flip_suppressed_hold_policy rejection rows on bybit_1 END at 2026-06-07 (ids 2516-2524, all 'desired long opposes current short qty=0.505'); ZERO vwap rejections after the #2982 deploy. The ~165 rejections/24h of bybit_1 journal noise has collapsed. vwap retains no live-money authority anywhere (execution:shadow + OFF in every regime cell), so the routing drop has zero execution-quality cost. Closed.
evidence_log: 2026-06-08 (/performance-review): STILL FIRING at the predicted cadence. /api/diag/journal?table=trades pull (issue #2974, window 2026-06-06->2026-06-08): 33 vwap rejections of 49 trade rows in the window, ALL on bybit_1 with reason 'REJECTED: intent_noop:flip_suppressed_hold_policy: desired long opposes current short'. bybit_2 (live) is clean — zero flip_suppressed rejections, pattern remains demo-only. items.title: trend_donchian on SPX: two-sided FAILS but the LONG side is a BTC-uncorrelated diversification candidate (+22.5 R long-only at donchian=20)
items.description: Tested whether the one durable roster winner (trend_donchian) transfers to an uncorrelated market (SPX/MES) for diversification. RESULT: two-sided trend_donchian is net-NEGATIVE on SPX at every tested lookback (dc=20 net -13.5R, dc=30 -14.2, dc=40 -29.1, dc=55 -29.9). BUT the entire loss is the SHORT side; the LONG side is net-POSITIVE at every lookback: long-only net R = +22.45 (dc=20), +16.69 (dc=30), +9.45 (dc=40), +7.20 (dc=55). SPX's secular uptrend systematically punishes trend-breakout shorts (short-side net ~ -31..-38R across configs). Shorter lookback is clearly best (dc=20: 278 long trades, long-side exp ~ +0.08 R/trade, net-positive in 2021/2023/2024; longer lookbacks degrade both sides). maxDD grows with lookback (27.6R @ dc=20 -> 39R @ dc=55). LEAD: a long-only / long-biased trend_donchian on SPX/MES is a plausible diversification add — uncorrelated to the BTC roster, exploits the equity risk premium, positive long-side expectancy. CAVEATS: in-sample over the available 6.4y; SPX500 CFD prices (live is MES — execution/contract differ); long-side R is the long legs of the two-sided run (entries are independent, so it equals a true long-only run, but a dedicated long-only + walk-forward + corr-to-BTC-trend_donchian check should confirm before any proposal). [WALK-FORWARD ADDED 2026-05-31 — see walk_forward field: long side positive IS+OOS but thin OOS edge; dc=30 most robust.]

items.walk_forward: Long-side walk-forward (SPX 2h, net-of-fee): dc=20 long-only IS(2020-2023)=+21.08R/169tr -> OOS(2024-2026)=+1.15R/109tr (nearly flat); dc=30 IS=+11.73R/158tr -> OOS=+4.73R/95tr (exp +0.05). QUALIFIED PASS: the long side stays POSITIVE in both windows (does not flip negative OOS) but the OOS edge is THIN, esp. dc=20; dc=30 holds up best proportionally. Read: a legitimate-but-MODEST BTC-uncorrelated diversification lead, not a slam-dunk. Short side negative in every window (confirmed). Before any Tier-3 proposal: build a true long-only adapter, validate on live MES (not SPX500 CFD) history, and measure return-correlation to the live BTC trend_donchian — the portfolio value is the low correlation even at a modest standalone edge.
items.mes_validation: ATTEMPTED on the live instrument (MES) and INCONCLUSIVE: MES market_raw on the VM spans only 2025-01-03 -> 2026-05-22 (~16 months). trend_donchian long-only on MES 2h is NEGATIVE (dc=20 long -52.4R, dc=30 long -46.1R, win ~23%) — but this does NOT refute the SPX thesis, because MES's entire window (2025-2026) coincides with the years that were ALSO negative for SPX trend_donchian long (SPX 2025/2026 legs were the drag in the full-period sweep). The SPX long-only edge originated in 2021/2023/2024 — years MES has no data for. NET: cannot validate (or refute) the long-only diversification thesis on the real tradeable instrument until MES history covers a trend-up regime. This is a HARD BLOCKER on maturing the lead — acquire deeper MES/ES history (or accept SPX500-CFD as the proxy with the known instrument mismatch) before any Tier-3 step.

items.title: htf_pullback_trend_2h: a tuned config (pullback_frac=0.5, trail_mult=5.0) PASSES net-of-fee + walk-forward on BTC 2h — genuine positive-expectancy diversification candidate
items.description: The scaffolded candidate htf_pullback_trend_2h (inert, in PR #2466) FAILS at default params (trend_lookback=50/pullback_frac=0.33/trail_mult=3.0 -> net -12.1 R over 5y). An 18-cell param sweep found a clear, CONSISTENT improvement: deeper pullback entry (pullback_frac=0.5) + WIDER trailing stop (trail_mult=5.0) flips it strongly positive at every trend_lookback tested (net +50.5/+52.3/+55.0 R at tl=30/50/80 over the full 5y). Root reason: the default trail_mult=3.0 was cutting the trend-follower's runners too early; a low-win-rate (~37%) high-R-multiple trend follower needs a wide trail to capture the rare big winners. WALK-FORWARD PASSED (the key test): at pullback_frac=0.5/trail_mult=5.0, tl=50 -> IS(2021-2023) net +32.7 R / OOS(2024-2026) net +22.4 R (159 trades, exp +0.14 R/trade, long-side +24.9 OOS); tl=80 -> IS +42.0 / OOS +14.9. Net-positive in BOTH windows at both lookbacks; tl=50 retains ~70% of its in-sample edge out-of-sample. This is the strongest candidate result of the session (session_breakout_trend is dead; SPX trend_donchian long-only is thin). CAVEATS: low win rate => higher variance / deeper equity swings; the +50R full-period figure is the best of 18 post-hoc configs (overfit risk on magnitude), but the walk-forward + cross-lookback consistency argue the SIGN and rough size are real; single market (BTC), single fee assumption.

items.title: trend_donchian live record is 0% win / 19 trades / -198R despite being the research-BEST strategy — almost certainly an execution/monitor bug, not a dead edge
items.description:
While gathering demotion evidence, trend_donchian (the program's strongest backtested edge: +43R 1h walk-forward, the shadow A/B trend_donchian_1h just validated) shows a LIVE record of 19 closed trades, 0 wins, net -198R (bybit_1 demo -196.87; real-money bybit_2 -1.56/4). A 0% win rate on the research-best strategy is a red flag for an EXECUTION/exit problem, not edge failure. Candidate causes to investigate: (a) the Chandelier ATR trail exit not being applied in the live order_monitor (every trade SL-hits before the trail can run), (b) the TP-sentinel / TP-distance issues tracked in PB-20260527-001 / PB-20260528-001, (c) flip-churn before FLIP_POLICY=hold shipped 2026-05-31 (check whether the losses pre-date the flip-policy default flip). Do NOT demote trend_donchian on this — it is the roster's edge; fix the execution. Cross-ref: the 1h shadow A/B (trend_donchian_1h) and mes_trend_long_1d both reuse the same trend unit and backtest strongly, which isolates the problem to the LIVE execution/monitor path of the 2h instance.

[2026-06-01 INVESTIGATION RESULT] Root-caused via the synced journal (issues #2542-#2547). NOT a dead edge and NOT a broken exit path: (a) reconciler_filled is the normal way a linear-perp exchange-side exit is journaled — fade (7/15 winners) and ict_scalp (8/11, +340) execute + win through the identical path; (b) the Chandelier trail DOES ratchet the live stop (squeeze 35/83 packages, trend_donchian 13/38). The 0%-win is substantially REGIME — trend_donchian + squeeze are breakout strategies and BTC ranged for the ~1-month live window (the channels logged are all ranges), so every breakout whipsawed. Two real execution bugs AMPLIFY it: (1) a re-entry storm — the open-package gate only blocks while a package is OPEN, so when one closes mid-bar the strategy re-fires within the same bar (9 packages in ~1h on a 2h strategy 2026-06-01), and the intent layer then no-ops the duplicate, flooding the journal with intent_noop rejection rows (969 for vwap, 106 squeeze) that skew per-strategy stats; (2) high orphan rates (trend 58%, vwap 63%, squeeze 29%, ict_scalp 31%) — positions open in DB then go flat on the exchange without a clean fill record. Fix (1) shipped as the bar-close debounce (this PR: _same_bar_entry_for_strategy in strategy_monocle.py + the pipeline gate). Fix (2) (orphan-rate root cause) tracked separately in health-review-backlog BL-20260601-001.

items.resolution_criteria: Re-entry storm eliminated (no >1 package per closed bar per strategy+symbol; intent_noop rejection rows drop sharply) after the bar-debounce deploys; and a fresh net-positive trend_donchian window once BTC trends again confirms the edge survives live. The orphan-rate half is BL-20260601-001.
items.resolution_note: ROOT-CAUSED + RESOLVED with full per-trade evidence (docs/research/trend-donchian-live-anomaly-rootcause-2026-07-11.md; trainer-vm-diag relays #6136/#6137/#6138 over the synced trade_journal.db, mtime 2026-07-11). VERDICT: the '0% win / -198R over 19 trades' was a MEASUREMENT ARTIFACT, NOT a dead edge and NOT a broken exit path. Decomposition (exact match to this item's own -196.87/-1.56/4 split): the -198 is 4 genuine reconciler_filled fills = -$196.87 DEMO (bybit_1 ids 1722/1731) + only -$1.56 REAL money (bybit_2 ids 1721/1732), all LONG breakouts that stopped out in the late-May BTC $75-78k RANGE (trend-breakout-in-chop, the regime study's known failure). The '19 trades' count was padded with ~15 NULL-pnl intent_reduce / reconciler_incomplete bookkeeping legs (re-entry storm) -> '0 of 19'. Two real execution defects present in that window are BOTH already fixed and BOTH produced NO real-money loss: (c) the TP-sentinel exchange-reject storm (ids 1747-1781, negative/>10% TP -> Bybit ErrCode 10001 -> rejected, no fills; fixed by _TP_SENTINEL_CAP_PCT=0.099 + long_only) and (c) the same-bar re-entry storm (fixed by #2548 bar-debounce). (b) reconciler/orphan artifacts are MINIMAL here: zero 'superseded' rows (the MGC-18-phantom class did NOT hit trend_donchian BTC), just a 4-row stuck_strategy_watchdog tail ~-$0.3. FORWARD (full window 2026-05-25 -> 07-10): net-POSITIVE on both accounts (bybit_1 demo +$1191 incl. 2039 short +$3870 / 3087 long +$2132; bybit_2 real +$4.77) -- edge intact. Regime vol-gate is NOT dropping signals (all 6 regime_hard_gate rows = gated:false/regime_allow_explicit/cell:on; long ON every regime); FLIP_POLICY=hold not implicated (losses predate 05-31, plain SL stop-outs). The real forward constraint is CAPITAL: bybit_2 is undercapitalized for BTC (24 sized_qty=0 refusals) so a real-money record can't accrue (PB-20260630-001 / BL-20260628-CRYPTO-INSTRUMENT-MIN-FLOOR). ACTIONS: KEEP LIVE (no demote); shipped a Tier-1 analytics guard (exclude_reduce_leg_predicate in _clean_trades.py, applied in /strategy/attribution) so a reduce leg can never again pad a win-rate denominator or inject a phantom win/loss; logged the deeper intent_reduce phantom-pnl reconciler accounting bug to the health-review backlog. The research->live pipeline is TRUSTWORTHY -- the failure was in READING the live record (counting demo money + bookkeeping legs as real trades), not in the edge or its execution today.

items.resolution_note: Resolved on the multiyear 5m archive (data/backtest_BTCUSDT_5m.csv, 647,585 bars, 2020-03 -> 2026-05) via the detached-runner relay pattern (#2575 kick-off / #2579 result; the synchronous follow-up #2576 was killed by an SSH-pipe drop at ~6min but the setsid+nohup runner was unaffected). The harness now threads the four LIVE EXIT-SIDE gates (config 51a18e8 + e367495's branch HEAD): four new CLI flags (--min-r-for-vwap-cross, --min-hold-minutes-for-vwap-cross, --be-at-r, --be-offset-bps) + a BE ratchet in _simulate_trade + _vwap_cross_gates_allow() mirroring the live vwap._vwap_cross_gates_pass. Defaults preserve v1 behaviour. SCOPE NOTE: recent_context_filter was intentionally NOT threaded — per config/strategies.yaml line 369 and src/runtime/strategy_signal_builders.py line 631 it is 'Informational only - does not block entries'; the starter-prompt's framing of it as a selectivity filter was inaccurate. The three exit-side gates are the actual live difference. RESULT: 40,650 trades over 6+ years, gross +3,399 R, fees -14,123 R, NET -10,724 R, win rate 49.8% (the high WR is the BE-ratchet locking in many sub-fee small wins). Per-regime: trending -6,179 (long -2,970 / short -3,209), transitional -1,903, chop -2,642 — exp ~ -0.26 R/trade in every regime. The gates DO work on the gross side (the unfiltered run was gross-negative too); the failure mode they target ('VWAP drifted to price') was real and they fix it. But the BE ratchet exits sooner via BE-stop hits, the cooldown shortens, trade count rises (~18/day gated vs ~11/day unfiltered), the fee per trade is unchanged, and the fee:gross ratio of ~4.2x swamps the strategy edge. Confirms docs/audits/vwap-viability-verdict-2026-05-23.md ('vwap has no net-of-fee edge') on the full multiyear window with the precise live-gate path. Matrix row + structural read updated; router policy table now gates vwap off in EVERY regime (long+short, all three regimes) — same shape as fvg but ~600x the magnitude. Matrix is now decision-grade for every roster strategy.

Phase 2 emits `event=regime_shadow_gate` audit rows with `enforced: false` for every OFF-cell intent the policy table (config/regime_policy.yaml) WOULD suppress; the aggregator's decision is unchanged. Phase 3 is the operator-approved Tier-3 step that turns the OFF cells into hard gates inside aggregate_intents(), behind a single `REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED` env-var with a one-flag rollback. Pre-conditions before opening the PR: (1) accrue at least a week of live shadow data (~7 days); (2) pull /api/diag/audit filtered to event=regime_shadow_gate, count per (strategy, regime, side); (3) cross-reference with the actual fills + closed PnL of those same intents — confirm the would-gate decisions correlate with the matrix's net-negative cells (vwap OFF everywhere, fvg OFF chop, trend-short OFF trending+transitional, htf_pullback OFF chop). If the live shadow data CONTRADICTS the matrix's verdict for any cell (e.g. vwap actually earned in trending despite the matrix saying -6,179 R), pause and re-investigate before turning the gate on. Implementation: in src/runtime/intents.py::aggregate_intents, drop OFF-cell candidates from `candidates` before the rest of the logic AND emit `event=regime_hard_gate` with `enforced: true`. Hidden behind `os.environ.get('REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED', '0')` — operator flips the env on the live VM via set-env operator action; rollback is one env flip + restart. Tests: every existing phase-2 test extended with an `enabled` parameter that exercises both the log-only (current) and hard-gate (phase-3) code paths.

evidence_log: 2026-06-01 (/performance-review, operator decision): OPTION 2 CHOSEN. Operator accepted the regime-roster MATRIX as sufficient evidence to proceed to phase-3 hard gates (behind REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED, one-flag rollback) rather than manufacturing live OFF-cell intents to grow the shadow soak (Option 1 explicitly rejected — it would mean placing -EV trades just to generate logging). RATIONALE confirmed in code: _shadow_regime_gate (intents.py:676) runs over ALL candidate intents BEFORE execution:shadow is resolved (multi_account_execute), so the would-gate stream IS accumulating — but it is dominated by vwap (execution:shadow), where a hard gate is REDUNDANT; no LIVE strategy currently sits in an OFF cell (trend_donchian + mes are long-only, long cells ON). So phase-3 gates are ~a no-op on today's live PnL and only bite once a two-sided strategy is re-promoted (e.g. squeeze PERF-20260601-005, or reclaiming trend's chop-short via a policy cell). PRE-CONDITION before flipping the flag: PERF-20260601-008 base-rate check must pass (live regime distribution ~= matrix base rates) — a detector/data mismatch would gate the wrong cells. Ongoing gate firing+performance monitoring tracked in PERF-20260601-011.

2026-06-07 (PERF-20260601-006 PHASE 3 PR SHIPPED): src/runtime/intents.py gains _hard_regime_gate() that DROPS OFF-cell intents from the aggregator's candidate set BEFORE reinforcement / conflict-resolution AND emits regime_hard_gate (enforced:true). New _regime_router_enabled() reads REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED env (default 0). aggregate_intents() routes to exactly one of _shadow_regime_gate (default) OR _hard_regime_gate (when enabled) — clean event-name partition between phase-2 history (regime_shadow_gate enforced:false) and phase-3 history (regime_hard_gate enforced:true). Fail-permissive on any policy load / verdict exception (keeps intent — never silently strands a live signal). 9 new tests in tests/test_aggregate_intents_regime_hard.py cover: default-off byte-identical to phase 2, off-cell drop + audit, on-cell keep + no audit, mixed drop+keep, all-off → flat, conflict-resolution-after-gate, empty-policy + load-exception fail-permissive, env-flag truthy-value parser. Pre-conditions met (soak ~6-7 days since #2583 deploy 6/1; base-rate check resolved 6/2 via audit_query). PR open as Tier-3 pending operator merge + env-flag flip; status moves resolved once flag flips on live VM and first regime_hard_gate row lands. Phase-4 (PERF-20260601-007) stays gated on phase-3 production lift evidence.

items.source: Regime router design doc § 5.4. Phase 4 is the soft-weight graduation after hard gates prove out.
items.title: Regime router phase 4 — graduate to soft weights + classifier-v0 detector [Tier-3]
items.description: Phase 4 (the design doc's last step): once phase-3 hard gates have demonstrated lift in production for >= a few weeks, graduate the policy from binary on/off to soft confidence weights (numeric cells like 0.5 / 1.0 / 1.5 multiply intent.confidence before priority resolution). The phase-2 evaluator already parses `weight: N.N` cells and routes them as permissive with a tagged reason — phase 4 wires the multiplier in. Also: validate `regime-classifier-baseline-v0` (the shadow regime model in the registry) against ADX-14 on the same per-tick stream; if its predictions correlate >0.85 with ADX-14 and add value at the regime boundaries (the 18.5-22.5 ADX hysteresis zone the design doc Q4 flagged), swap the detector source from 'adx-14' to 'classifier-v0' in src/runtime/regime/detector.py (the audit-row `regime_source` field already supports this; consumer is forward-compatible).
items.trigger_condition: After phase-3 hard gates have been live for >= 2-3 weeks with measurable lift; OR when the ML lifecycle promotes regime-classifier-baseline-v0 past shadow.

items.description: After PR #2583 deploys, the trader emits two new audit fields on every `*_eval` row (`regime`, `adx_14`, `regime_source`) and a new `regime_shadow_gate` event for every OFF-cell intent. Verify against the matrix's expected base rates: pull /api/diag/audit?limit=2000 across a 24-48h window after deploy, group by strategy + regime, confirm the distribution matches the matrix (BTC 1h: chop ~30% / transitional ~19% / trending ~51%; BTC 5m: chop ~35% / transitional ~20% / trending ~45%). If a strategy's regime distribution diverges from the matrix by >5 percentage points in any bucket, investigate (could be candles-fetch mismatch, detector bug, or a regime shift in BTC since the matrix was measured). Cross-tab the regime_shadow_gate event counts against the matrix's OFF cells — vwap should dominate the shadow rows (it fires on every 5m bar regardless of regime; OFF in all 3 regimes). Tier-1 (autonomous diag pulls + arithmetic; no live-path change). Resolution: a short note in docs/research/ or a backlog comment that the live regime stream matches the matrix; gate PERF-20260601-006 (phase 3) on this passing.

items.source: docs/research/regime-router-design-2026-06-01.md § 6 Q3 — operator deferred the long_only retire decision to a phase-3 follow-up ("leave that for now").
items.title: Retire the trend_donchian long_only flag once the regime router policy table subsumes it [Tier-3, follow-up to phase 3]
items.description: trend_donchian's `long_only: true` flag in config/strategies.yaml was shipped as #2570 (2026-06-01) as a simple, robust win that dropped the strategy's -37 R short drag. The regime router's policy table independently mirrors the same constraint: trend_donchian.short is OFF in trending+transitional (the regimes where the -52 R loss concentrates) and ON in chop (the +16 R chop-short edge). Once phase 3 (PERF-20260601-006) is live and the table is enforcing those gates, the strategy-level long_only flag becomes redundant — both flags would be saying the same thing in trending/transitional, AND the table is the ONLY way to recover the chop-short edge. The cleanup is: flip `long_only: false` (or remove the field entirely) on trend_donchian in config/strategies.yaml AND remove the `_short_suppressed_long_only` branch in src/units/strategies/trend_donchian.py / strategy_signal_builders.py. The router policy table will then be the single source of truth for trend_donchian's direction gating. Operator's instruction 2026-06-01: "leave that for now" — keep long_only as defense-in-depth until phase 3 proves out the policy-table enforcement. Mechanical risk if NOT cleaned up after phase 3: when an operator-approved exception manually flips a cell in regime_policy.yaml (e.g. enable trend_donchian chop-short in some future regime study), the YAML-level long_only flag will silently override and suppress that intent — a confusing footgun.
items.trigger_condition: After PERF-20260601-006 (regime router phase 3 hard gates) has been live for >= 1-2 weeks with no regression in trend_donchian's fill rate or PnL.
items.resolution_criteria: config/strategies.yaml::trend_donchian no longer carries `long_only: true`; the `short_suppressed_long_only` branch is removed from the signal builder; the existing test_trend_donchian_long_only.py is either deleted or rewritten to verify the SAME behaviour is produced via the regime router policy table cells. Operator-approved Tier-3 PR.

items.source: /performance-review 2026-06-01 — order_packages pull showed vwap AND htf_pullback_trend_2h emitting confidence=1.0 on every package.
items.title: Degenerate signal confidence: vwap + htf_pullback_trend_2h emit confidence=1.0 on EVERY order package — carries no information for gating/sizing/ML
items.description: In the 2026-06-01 window every vwap (17/17) and htf_pullback_trend_2h (2/2) order package carried confidence=1.0 exactly, while trend_donchian emitted a genuine graded value (0.392). A confidence hard-pinned at 1.0 is degenerate: it can't drive a min-confidence floor, can't size by conviction, and feeds the shadow ML models (setup_quality etc.) a constant feature. Likely the signal builders for these two strategies set confidence=1.0 as a placeholder rather than computing it from setup strength (e.g. vwap: distance-in-sigma from the band; htf: pullback depth / trend strength). NOT a live-capital bug today (vwap+htf are execution:shadow) but it (a) blunts any future min-confidence gate, (b) pollutes the ML feature, (c) makes per-decision grading rely on geometry alone. CHECK src/runtime/strategy_signal_builders.py for the vwap + htf confidence assignment.
items.trigger_condition: Next /performance-review; or when wiring a min-confidence floor or confidence-weighted sizing for vwap/htf; or when reviewing ML feature quality.
items.resolution_criteria: vwap and htf_pullback_trend_2h order packages carry a confidence that VARIES with setup strength (not a constant 1.0); OR it's confirmed by-design that these strategies don't expose a graded confidence and the placeholder is documented + excluded from ML features.

Still open.

2026-06-06 /performance-review: RE-CONFIRMED AGAIN. All 18 in-window vwap order_packages (journal #2872, 6/5 22:37 -> 6/6 06:21Z) carry confidence exactly 1.0 (entry/sl/tp vary, conf does not). The shadow log feature_row for vwap-driven scores also shows confidence:1.0. Unchanged hardcoded constant; blunts any min-confidence gate + pollutes the ML setup_quality feature. Still open. Cheapest fix remains: derive vwap confidence from deviation-in-sigma (the std_dev + deviation_std are already in signal_logic).

items.description: Standing check the strategy review runs each pass, in two layers. (1) FIRING CORRECTNESS (phase 2+): pull a window where actionable directional intents fired (e.g. a vwap-active period) and confirm regime_shadow_gate events appear with the EXPECTED (strategy, side, regime, cell) per config/regime_policy.yaml + the regime-roster matrix, enforced:false in phase 2. Confirm the would-gate counts are sane (not zero when OFF-cell intents clearly fired; not gating ON-cell or unlisted strategies). NOTE the gate is event-driven on actionable intents, so a quiet window legitimately shows few/none — sample a busy window. (2) GATE PERFORMANCE: tally would-gate counts per (strategy, regime, side) vs ACTUAL fills, and once phase 3 is live (REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED) verify the hard gates (a) are actually suppressing the OFF-cell intents they should, (b) are NOT suppressing winners — cross-check the regime-tagged decisions' realized PnL by regime against the matrix's expected sign, and flag any OFF cell that would have been net-POSITIVE live (regime shift since the matrix was measured) or any ON cell bleeding. Escalate a divergence >5pp on base rates (-> PERF-20260601-008) or a sign-flip on any cell as a tweak proposal / new backlog item.

updates.note: 2026-06-23: Phase-0 re-study confirmed FX majors FAIL trend_donchian at validated params (EURUSD 1h -28.5R train; only 4h weakly +), so a live re-point is a per-pair TUNING project needing a fresh trainer-VM backtest (sandbox can't run it: no FX data, egress blocked). Operator chose (2026-06-23) to RUN the trainer-VM FX backtest sweep. Sweep kicked off on the trainer (autonomous): fetch EUR/JPY/GBP/AUD/CAD majors, screening trend(1h/4h)+pullback(2h) net-of-fee train/OOS + a bounded param grid, then the full validation gate on any robust-both-windows cell. Outcome -> either a paper_ready wiring PR (exact diffs already drafted) or 'no FX cell clears -> keep shelved'. Stays open pending the sweep verdict.

SWEEP COMPLETE (trainer VM, 2026-06-23, operator-approved). Screened 5 OANDA-US majors (EUR/GBP/JPY/AUD/CAD) x trend(1h/4h)+pullback(2h) x a param grid (donchian 15/20/30, atr_stop 2.0/2.5/3.0, trail 3.0/3.5, min_conf 0/.05/.1) x train(<2025)+OOS(2025+) net-of-fee at 2.0 AND 3.0 bps RT = 1350 cells. RESULT: 12 cells net-positive in BOTH windows at 2.0bps (best USDCAD trend/4h +3.1 train /+2.7 OOS; USDJPY pullback +5.4/+1.8; EURUSD trend/4h +1.3/+1.9) but ZERO survive the 3.0bps fee stress. The positives are tiny (~+2.7R OOS / ~62 trades / 6mo) and fee-fragile, evaporating within the realistic OANDA-major spread band (2-3bps RT). A k-fold validation gate (requires 2x-fee headroom) would reject all, so it was not run. VERDICT: no FX cell clears the robustness bar -> KEEP the OANDA sleeve SHELVED (strategies:[], dry_run); XAU stays unrouted (orphan noise already solved). Confirms the prior Phase-0 rejection of FX majors. Revisit only if a NEW strategy type (e.g. carry/cross-sectional) changes the thesis. Evidence: trainer /home/ubuntu/fx-sweep/out/screen_results.csv; relay issues #4261/#4264/#4269/#4278.

items.title: Verify ict_scalp_5m live edge — net-negative in 3-yr prop backtest
items.source: The Breakout prop-POC evaluation added ict_scalp_5m to the portfolio engine + Monte-Carlo rig (runtime_logs/prop_eval/2026-06-16-expanded/). Solo backtest over the qashdev BTCUSDT 5m feed (2023-01->2026-02): 474 trades, 36.9% win rate, 148 TP vs 324 SL, net ~ -93% on a $5k account — below the ~40% break-even for its 1.5R target. It also poisons otherwise-passing combos when netted into a shared position.
items.description: ict_scalp_5m is LIVE on real money (bybit_2). This 3-yr backtest shows a clear negative edge. The backtest is a netted-portfolio sim and may not reflect live ML/regime gating, recent param tuning, or the live confidence filter — so verify against ACTUAL live closed trades before acting. If the negative edge is confirmed live, propose demote-to-shadow or a parameter review (Tier-3); if live materially differs, document why the backtest under-represents it.
items.resolution_criteria: A /performance-review compares ict_scalp_5m's live closed-trade PnL + win rate (real money, bybit_2) against this backtest. Outcome is either (a) confirmed net-negative live -> Tier-3 proposal to demote/shadow or retune, or (b) live differs -> documented reconciliation of why.

items.title: trend_donchian is +EV on Bybit high-vol alts (SOL/ETH/BNB) — prop candidate validated + WIRED (SOL live long-only, ETH shadow); soak follow-ups remain (Tier-3)
items.source: Cost-aware EV sweep runtime_logs/prop_eval/2026-06-16-bybit-multisymbol/. trend_donchian (a BTC loser) is strongly +EV on every high-vol alt, out-of-sample (BTC-tuned, no per-symbol re-tuning), top cell @1.5% risk for all 5: SOL +$1,707/12mo (95% P(net>0), 9.5x ROI on fees), ETH +$1,183 (93%), BNB +$1,101 (92%), DOGE +$587 (80%), XRP +$576 (76%) on the $5k Breakout 1-Step ruleset. Works via bank-ASAP economics (breaches+rebuys ~4-6 accounts/yr but banks more than fees first). First genuine +EV prop candidate found; ~4-5x the BTC baseline.
items.description: This is research on Binance Vision SPOT 5m (Bybit-perp proxy, ~0-10bps basis) with the EV model's realised-only optimism. Before any live wiring (Tier-3, operator-gated): (a) re-validate on REAL Bybit perp candles + funding, (b) per-alt walk-forward to confirm the trend edge isn't a single-window artifact (the 5/5 consistency argues real), (c) pick execution path (prop_signal/Comet manual bridge for supervised soak, or MetaCopier unattended). Best risk-adjusted cell: SOL @1.0% (+$1,018, 87%, 11x ROI, only ~2 accounts/yr).
items.resolution_criteria: Either trend_donchian-on-an-alt is validated on real Bybit perp data + walk-forward and the operator approves a Tier-3 wire onto the prop account, OR the edge fails revalidation (perp basis / funding / OOS) and is documented as not live-worthy.

items.validation_result: RE-VALIDATED on real Bybit LINEAR-PERP 5m (2023-01..2026-02, 332k bars/sym) with the Breakout venue cost model (flat ~0.09%/day daily SWAP — perp-futures-style sim, NOT Bybit 8h funding) + a 4-fold per-alt walk-forward. Harness: scripts/prop/validate_alt_prop.py + src/prop/funding.py (run on trainer VM). GATE result: SOL = robust PASS (pre-swap realised +$2,150; 15% drag; EV@1.5% +$1,693, 94% P>0; 4/4 OOS folds). ETH = marginal PASS (57% swap drag eats the edge; pre +$671 -> post +$285; EV@1.5% +$1,050; 4/4 folds). BNB = label-only PASS — realised ledger NEGATIVE pre & post swap (pre -$37 -> post -$524); the +EV is EV-model renewable-account optimism only -> EXCLUDED. Recommendation: wire SOL (and optionally ETH as shadow); drop BNB. Results: runtime_logs/prop_eval/2026-06-17-validate-real/.
items.shipped: WIRED + DEPLOYED live (operator-approved). PR #3840 (executor + breakout_1 prop account + alt variants), PR #3848 (directional A/B → SOL long-only / ETH two-sided + builder enforcement). A Breakout daily-swap long-only A/B (runtime_logs/prop_eval/2026-06-17-validate-real/, --long-only) split the variants: SOL HOLDS long-only (pre-swap +$1,325 -> post +$1,158; EV@1.5% +$1,131, 86% P>0; 4/4 folds, cleaner dispersion) -> trend_donchian_sol = execution:live + long_only:true; ETH's edge is short-side-dependent (long-only FLIPS it negative: pre +$23 -> post -$181; EV@1.5% +$415, 66%, 3/4 folds, fold-1 -$166) -> trend_donchian_eth = execution:shadow, two-sided. breakout_1 is account_class:prop, mode:live (always-on Telegram/FCM ping; operator decides per-signal whether to place — no broker API). BNB EXCLUDED. Deployed via pull-and-deploy (issue #3849, b2afabb->cdb4f8b, ict-trader-live active; post-deploy journal confirms trend_donchian_eth evaluating live).
follow_ups: SOL + ETH LIVE-TICKET SOAK: both variants are now execution:live (ETH promoted from shadow 2026-06-17, PR #3852). Confirm each emits prop_signal tickets on real breakouts (Telegram/FCM to the prop bot) — SOL LONG-only (no shorts), ETH two-sided — and that they journal as prop-manual-<uuid> order packages WITHOUT a phantom exchange position the monitor would orphan-close. Watch ETH especially: it's the marginal one (57% swap drag), so review its live-ticket cadence + whether the operator's manual placements track the EV.

ETH SHADOW->LIVE: DONE 2026-06-17 (operator-approved, PR #3852) — promoted to live as a supervised ping (operator gates each placement; reversible to shadow with one flip). Now a soak-and-watch item rather than a pending decision.

SWAP-RATE — OPERATOR DELEGATED TO CLAUDE (2026-06-17). DECISION: keep the 0.0009/day (~0.09%/day) Breakout daily swap as the working model assumption — it is the conservative third-party-review figure and the validation PASSED at it. It does NOT gate the live POC because prop execution is supervised Telegram pings (no automated capital sizing). REMAINING (non-blocking): confirm the actual per-symbol swap from the Breakout/DXTrade instrument spec and re-run the gate if it differs materially (ETH is sensitive at 57% drag); until confirmed, keep real-capital sizing modest. The harness takes it as the --swap-rate-daily knob.
BNB / DOGE / XRP: documented NOT live-worthy at the Breakout daily-swap cost (BNB realised-negative; DOGE/XRP not re-validated on real perp). Revisit only if the venue cost model changes.

items.title: Cross-sectional (rank-N long/short) sleeve un-built — deprioritized after pairs/stat-arb rejected
items.description: A cross-sectional rank-N long/short sleeve (rank a universe by a factor, long the top / short the bottom) was scoped but NOT built. It was deprioritized after the pairs / statistical-arbitrage relative-value approaches were rejected — ETH/BTC + GLD/SLV + TLT/IEF pairs all FAILED the robustness gate, indicating the available edge is directional (trend/pullback), not relative-value. Cross-sectional is a sibling relative-value family, so the rejection of pairs lowers its prior. Revisit only if a future study surfaces a genuine cross-sectional factor edge. Evidence: docs/research/expansion-backtesting-research-2026-06-20.md § 0e.

items.source: Operator follow-up 2026-06-26 + docs/audits/order-packages-zero-qty-2026-06-26.md § Follow-up. Diag #4654: every bybit_1 paper close in-window (2847,2854-2860,2867,2894) is an intent_reduce/reconciler_filled leg, not a clean entry->exit round-trip; alpaca_paper rows dominated by netting-guard/hold-policy suppressions.
items.title: Bucket artifact-heavy paper records: gradeable round-trips vs technical artifacts vs broker-truncated-reconstructable
items.description: Per-strategy performance is distorted by paper records that are NOT clean strategy round-trips: intent-layer reduce/flip legs (intent_reduce=true), netting-guard/hold-policy suppressions (reentry_suppressed_*/flip_suppressed_*), reconciler_filled closes with no classifiable bracket reason, and orphan/re-adopt flaps. Proposed taxonomy: (A) GRADEABLE round-trips = open that reached a genuine sl/tp or strategy monitor() exit -> the ONLY rows that drive win-rate/expectancy; (B) TECHNICAL ARTIFACTS (reduce/flip/netting/hold/unclassified-reconciler/orphan-flap/creds-refusal) -> route to the technical/health backlog, exclude from the strategy scorecard; (C) BROKER-TRUNCATED-RECONSTRUCTABLE = trade closed mid-flight by reconciler/stuck-watchdog but we have entry+SL+TP -> keep the decision (entry quality, R:R) and reconstruct the would-be outcome by replaying /api/bot/candles from entry forward (first-touch SL vs TP within the bracket) -> label reconstructed_win/reconstructed_loss/open_at_window_end. Build as a performance-review pre-filter (+ optional reconstruction pass) so aggregates exclude bucket B by construction. Analysis/tooling layer, NOT a live-path change. Recommend operator OK to build it next; affects how every per-strategy number is computed.
items.trigger_condition: Next performance-review run, or when the operator approves building the bucketing pre-filter.
items.resolution_criteria: A performance-review step that classifies each closed paper record into bucket A/B/C, excludes B from per-strategy win-rate/expectancy, and reconstructs C outcomes from candles; documented + applied to one review.

updates.note: BUILT the bucketing tool: src/analysis/paper_record_classifier.py (pure A/B/C classifier) + src/analysis/trade_reconstruction.py (pure first-touch SL/TP reconstruction + candle adapter) + scripts/analysis/classify_paper_records.py (CLI, --json/--db, --reconstruct) + tests (21 passing). Validated on the real 2026-06-26 window (issue #4654, 48 recs): 0 gradeable / 46 artifact / 2 reconstructable. Wired into performance-review SKILL.md (run pre-filter before aggregates; compute over bucket-A only + bucket-C reconstructed). Shipped on branch claude/order-packages-zero-qty-eaofnv / PR #4660. REMAINING to close: apply it in one live performance-review run and confirm the per-strategy A/B/C split + reconstructed outcomes land in the report.
